intro: A plain roundup of the week's notable military and business technology (full analysis was unavailable this run).

deep_dives.title: Saronic Launches First Mirage 16-Meter Uncrewed Surface Vessel
deep_dives.what_happened: Saronic on July 2, 2026 announced the launch of its first Mirage, a 52-ft. (16-meter) dual-use Autonomous Surface Vessel (ASV) that joins the 24-ft. (7-meter) Corsair and 180-ft. (55-meter) Marauder as the third flagship platform in Saronic’s growing fleet, and is the latest milestone in a production model delivering autonomous vessels at speed and scale. ... The post Saronic Launches First Mirage 16-Meter Uncrewed Surface Vessel appeared first on Naval News .

deep_dives.title: US withdrew forces from Nigeria after operation against ISIS, AFRICOM chief says
deep_dives.what_happened: AFRICOM Commander Gen. Dagvin Anderson described May’s joint U.S.-Nigerian as a model for future security cooperation in Africa.

deep_dives.title: Hardened Aircraft Shelters At Russian Air Base In Crimea Damaged From Ukrainian Drone Strikes (Updated)
deep_dives.what_happened: The drone attacks on Saki Air Base are part of Ukraine's latest campaign to make Crimea as uninhabitable for Russian forces as possible. deep_dives.title: This Is The Massive Aerial Armada That Is About To Fly Over The Capital
deep_dives.what_happened: NASA will lead waves of aircraft, from stealth bombers to VIP helicopters, over the Mall to celebrate America's 250th birthday. The post This Is The Massive Aerial Armada That Is About To Fly Over The Capital appeared first on TWZ .

deep_dives.title: Royal Navy’s Sea Launch Of Combat-Proven Nyan Kamikaze Drone Points To Fleet’s ‘Hybrid’ Future
deep_dives.what_happened: The demonstration of a new shipboard strike capability comes amid advancing plans to integrate drones deeply across the fleet. The post Royal Navy’s Sea Launch Of Combat-Proven Nyan Kamikaze Drone Points To Fleet’s ‘Hybrid’ Future appeared first on TWZ .

deep_dives.title: U.S. Navy Eyes CPS Deployment from New VLS, Containers
deep_dives.what_happened: The U.S. Navy’s next-generation undersea developmental efforts aim to drastically expand the deployment methods from which the service’s Conventional Prompt Strike (CPS) hypersonics can be launched from and increase their production rates. Headed by the Portfolio Acquisition Executive Strategic Systems Programs, the service’s organization responsible for the development and sustainment of Submarine-Launched Ballistic Missiles and ... The post U.S. Navy Eyes CPS Deployment from New VLS, Containers appeared first on Naval News .

deep_dives.title: Multibillion-dollar contract secures ‘major step forward’ for GCAP fighter jet
deep_dives.what_happened: Japan, Italy and the U.K. have handed a £4.6 billion ($6.1 billion) development contract to their national champions building the GCAP jet.
